stem: A fitness studio charges members according to the function $F(m) = 30m + 50$, where $F(m)$ is the total amount paid, in dollars, after $m$ months of membership. How much will a member have paid in total after 4 months?
explanation: Substitute $m = 4$ into $F(m) = 30m + 50$: $F(4) = 30(4) + 50 = 120 + 50 = 170$. After 4 months, the member will have paid a total of $\$170$.

distractorRationale.A: Computes only the variable portion $30 \times 4 = 120$, forgetting to add the initial fee of $50$.
distractorRationale.B: Adds the monthly rate and the flat fee before multiplying: $(30 + 50) \times \frac{4}{\text{something}}$; specifically, computes $30 \times 4 = 120$ and then adds only $30$ instead of $50$: $120 + 30 = 150$.
distractorRationale.D: Uses $m = 5$ instead of $m = 4$: $30(5) + 50 = 150 + 50 = 200$, an off-by-one substitution error.
